title: Make sure we set the buildah version label
body:
Currently if the buildah image has a label that was built with
an older version of buildah, we don't update to the latest version.

This PR Will cause the new version to use the current label.

Fixes:https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3035

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM. I confirmed that this fixes the issue, and also confirmed that your new test is correct: the command-line `--label` reproduces the original problem on buildah @ current master. Thanks for addressing this so quickly!

While you're in here and it looks like you're going to need to adjust this to fix a test, can you update the comments in buildah.go and contrib/rpm/bulidah.spec please?  The line before the version line in each file has a comment that acts as a reminder to fix the other file.  Please update (don't remove) those with the correct files now.
@TomSweeneyRedHat did you accidentally press the `Close` button, or was that intentional?
Judgment call: I'm going to assume that was an accident, and I will reopen so we can see CI results.
@edsantiago good judgment, I did accidentally close it FWIW.  TY for reopening.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I actually did not change the version of buildah here. This PR is just changing the place where buildah sets a label in an image with the current version of buildah.
